div.head: 概述
div.p: 73號窟位於相同水平面的E區五窟中間位置（）。d壁與e壁刊刻六品《合部〈金光明經〉》。由於後部山體壓力，d壁一大部分向前推移，導致刻經錯位。e壁未滿刻，刻經止於第六品。f壁鎸刻千佛浮雕造像。

div.head: 位置

list.item: 位於南崖E區。高於現今步道約3.6米。
方位：f壁3˚。

div.p: 窟底較之西側72號窟高出19厘米，兩窟之間距離89厘米。與東側74號窟之間底部距離75厘米，頂部距離105厘米。

div.head: 形制
div.p: 敞口，標準方形窟，左側（東側）與門楣有雙層窟口。窟底平面呈梯形，進深大於窟寬（）。窟寬由前至後漸增。窟底與窟頂由前至後向上擡升。

list.item: d壁：前端221厘米高、後端220厘米高，260厘米寬。
e壁：中部222厘米高，223厘米寬。
f壁：前端222厘米高、後端220厘米高，265厘米寬。
窟口：214厘米寬。

div.head: 改造
div.p: 73窟是保存最好的石窟之一。d壁有所錯位，窟口有5至20厘米的坍塌部分。

figure.head: 73號窟平面圖。雷魯卡根據馬本漢和王平國田野繪圖製作
Ground plan of cave 73; drawing by Lukas Ledderose after field map by MB and MS

div.head: 布局

list.item: 打磨面尺寸爲192（前）－202（後）×248厘米，始於距窟頂7至12厘米處，終於距地面5至12厘米處，距後壁11至15厘米。窟口刻經面前端應有一窄小的打磨面。
刻經面爲182×236厘米的横長方形（包括不可見的前4行）。第5行距地面大約43厘米處有“十方”二字可見。727年的題記横向刊刻於刻經第31至43行上方。刻經距窟底16至26厘米，距後壁18.5至23厘米。刻經第二卷起始，即第106行起，無水平格綫，行距與字距變得緊密，每十字之間的長度縮短。

div.p: 刻經之後的第111行另有一則陀羅尼咒題。字體大於刻經，單字高2.5至3.0厘米，寬2.4至3.4厘米。

row.cell: 約0.1至1.0厘米，第106行之後間距漸小
第44行之前，每行83字；第45至105行，每行88字；第106行後字數增加，第119行達120字。
15.0至20.2厘米（最後一行，下方三分之一部分）
約0.0至1.0厘米，後五行較爲緊密

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: “之”字形裂縫從窟底至窟頂貫穿整個壁面，始於距後壁60厘米窟底處，近乎垂直地延伸至距窟底120厘米、後壁75厘米處，以水泥填補加固。裂縫自此水平向右繼續延伸130厘米，并就此蜿蜒直至窟頂。此裂縫最後一段亦以水泥填補，12厘米高，於窟口處形成一梯形區，底寬67厘米，上寬40厘米。
開裂右側下方墻體向窟口方向推移大概一行經文大小的寬度。由於壁面保存狀况較差，經文在窟口處推移區域難以辨識，但5-B到13-B區域的第32至87行可見。此區域的刻經下方皆稍向右偏移。在本卷中，我們采用黑色箭頭對此進行標記。
近窟口處窟頂下方梯形區域中有一橢圓形的孔洞，距窟底約155至160厘米。於4-A區域，刻經區外有一人工鑿洞，位於窟頂下方，距窟口55至60厘米。f壁上有相應鑿洞，距窟口78至85厘米。

row.cell: ［《金光明經》《序品》第一 一］：今已磨滅，由第106行推測復原。標識《合部〈金光明經〉》第一品（《序品》）的起始，亦是卷一的起始。
［《金光明經》《壽量品》第二］：今已磨滅，由第四品推測復原。標識《合部〈金光明經〉》卷一第二品《壽量品》的起始。
《金光明經》《三身分别品》［第三］：標識《合部〈金光明經〉》卷一第三品《三身分别品》的起始。
《金光明經》《懺悔品》第四：標識《合部〈金光明經〉》卷一第四品《懺悔品》的起始（此品爲《大正藏》本第二卷第一品）。
《金光明經》卷第一：標識《合部〈金光明經〉》卷一的結束，同時亦是卷一第四品《懺悔品》的結束。
《金光明經》業障滅品第五 二：標識《合部〈金光明經〉》第二卷的起始，亦是第五品《業障滅品》的起始。
自作教他見作隨喜身三：d壁《合部〈金光明經〉》第五品《業障滅品》最後的刻字。
佛頂尊勝陀羅［尼咒〕：刻經之後接一則陀羅尼咒題。

list.item: 打磨面約200×220厘米大小，從與d壁相鄰的角落起始，距窟頂7至9厘米，距窟底12至14厘米，并延伸至f壁。
刻經面爲竪長方形狀，高寬爲190×137厘米。約起始於距d壁11至20厘米、距窟頂14至17厘米處，最底下一排距窟底地面約15至18厘米，距f壁64厘米。
如同d壁的末五行，横排未對齊，但字距與行距不似d壁末端那般緊密。
刻經前有兩行殘損的題記，作於733年。

row.cell: 上方有部分縱細格綫可見
不等，第2行110字，第20行94字，第63行97字

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 表面平整，大部分區域風化，僅有刻經的上半部分可見。左側近窟頂處，刻經面以外有水漬。另有數處淺顯刻痕。

row.cell: 口四意三業行……：續刻自d壁第110行。
《金光明經》《贊嘆品》第六：標識《合部〈金光明經〉》第二卷第六品《贊嘆品》的起始（《大正藏》本第四卷第七品）。
……今汝二子銀相等是：第六品《贊嘆品》的最後刻字。

list.item: 滿壁雕刻千佛。

div.head: 討論
div.bibl:
彭家勝第7頁、第11頁
彭家勝提供了測量數據，測量該窟體積爲11.868立方米。作者辨識d壁刻經爲《金光明經》，將《檀三藏經》作爲獨立經文列出。作者還移録了d壁開元十五年（727）題記和e壁開元二十一年（733）題記。他的以上信息由胡文和提供。

何恩之第41頁
何恩之跟隨彭家勝的研究，同樣將《檀三藏經》視作一部獨立的佛經。

曹丹第51頁
曹丹抄録了題記。

胡文和第9－11頁
胡文和給出的該窟尺寸的測量數據同彭家勝（），但未計算其體積。他指出d壁所刻爲《合部〈金光明經〉》第三、第四和第五品，而e壁因爲損毁嚴重未能辨識經文。他還計量了經文完整處的單行平均字數，并對《合部〈金光明經〉》的合集性質作了簡要介紹。胡文和還移録了位於d壁上方作於727年的題記，并指出“檀”字出自梵語“ dāna ”一詞，意爲“布施”。

鄧之金第36頁
鄧之金辨識d壁刻經爲《金光明經》第五品，e壁刻經爲第六品，并提及d壁上方作於727年的題記。

胡文和第64頁、第68頁
胡文和重複其出版（）的説法。

李良和鄧之金第44頁
李良和鄧之金提供了該窟及各壁的測量數據。作者統計了刻經行數及字數，辨識出刻於d壁的《金光明經》第五品及e壁的第六品。此文亦提及d壁上方作於727年的題記。

note.p: 曾德仁 移録了作於727年的題記。

div.bibl:
《成都考古發現》第388頁、第407頁
成都文物考古研究所在其文章中仔細描述了石窟的建築形制，并提供所有窟壁的數據。文章辨識出d壁及e壁《合部〈金光明經〉》六品中各品的行數，并與《大正藏》本作比對。該文亦移録了作於727年及733年的兩則題記。

李琛妍第42頁、第50頁、第74頁註24
李琛妍將此73號窟納入其第V組（本卷E區）予以討論。作者提及了《金光明經》以及開元年間的題記，然而作者根據彭家勝和胡文和的研究，誤將題記中所言《檀三藏經》視作一部經書。

曹丹和于春第13頁
曹丹和于春提供了各壁以及其刻經面的數據，根據拓片計算現存經文字數：d壁，8188字；e壁，7521字。作者提到d壁上的陀羅尼，并移録了作於727年有關供養刻經的題記。作者辨識出e壁所刻爲《金光明經》第六品，并提及733年的題記。

秦臻、張雪芬和雷玉華第98－100頁、第124－125頁、第416－433頁
在此詳盡的關於卧佛院的研究中，作者重複成都文物考古研究所的測量數據（），同時提供了d壁及e壁立面圖，移録了全部刻經，用其方法系統標示了文本。作者同時辨識了异體字及刻經與《大正藏》本的异同。

肥田路美第109－110頁
肥田路美描述了該窟的大致情况。作者辨識d壁所存刻經出自《合部〈金光明經〉》前五品，并提到經文後面的陀羅尼咒。作者提到e壁繼刻的經文，并指出與《大正藏》本相比對，此處第六品與第七品順序倒置。作者移録d壁横刻的727年的供養題記。

鄧星亮第78頁、第101頁
鄧星亮提供了測量數據并大致描述了該窟，另對f壁的“千佛”造像作了描述。作者亦抄録了727年的題記。作者辨識d壁刻經出自《合部〈金光明經〉》卷一和卷二的一部分，移録了刻經起始與結束部分。作者提及e壁對第二卷的繼續刊刻，并認爲第三卷部分經文同樣被刊刻，亦移録了刻經起始與結束部分。

div.head: Overview
div.p: Cave 73 is the central cave in the horizontal row of five caves in section E (fig. ). Walls d and e are engraved with six chapters of the Synoptic Golden Light Sutra (Hebu Jinguangming jing 合部金光明經; hereafter: Golden Light Sutra). Due to pressure from the mountain behind, a large part of wall d has been pushed forward, causing a shift in the text columns. Wall e is only used in part; the engraving ends with chapter 6. Wall f has a relief of the Thousand Buddhas.

div.head: Location

list.item: Southern slope, section E, ca. 3.6 m above the walkway.
Direction of wall f: 3˚.

div.p: The floor of the cave is 19 cm higher than that of cave 72 beside it to the west. The wall separating the two caves is 89 cm thick; the wall separating it from cave 74 to the east is 75 cm thick near the base and 105 cm above.

div.head: Shape
div.p: Box-shaped, standard-sized, with open front and a reveal on the left (eastern) side of the doorway and in the lintel above. The rectangular ground plan is deeper than it is wide and widens towards the rear; both floor and ceiling rise towards the rear.

list.item: Wall d: height at the front 211 cm, height at the rear 220 cm, width 260 cm.
Wall e: height at midpoint 222 cm, width 223 cm.
Wall f: height at the front 222 cm, height at the rear 220 cm, width 265 cm.
Width of entrance: 214 cm.

div.head: Changes
div.p: Cave 73 is one of the best preserved caves except for the shift in wall d described below. At the entrance, 5 to 20 cm of wall d are broken off.

div.head: Layout

list.item: A polished area, 192 (front)–202 (rear) × 248 cm, begins 7–12 cm below the ceiling; it ends 5–12 cm above the floor and 11–15 cm from the rear wall. Probably a narrow polished area, which has broken off, framed the field of script next to the entrance.

The field of script is a horizontal rectangle, 182 × 236 cm (including the first four missing columns at the front). In column 5, only the two characters shifang 十方 are visible, ca. 43 cm above the floor. The colophon of 727 is engraved in a horizontal row above columns 31 through 43 of the sutra text.
The field of script ends 16–26 cm above the floor and 18.5–23 cm from the rear wall. Beginning with scroll 2 of the text, in column 106, horizontal rows of the grid pattern are no longer observed; columns and characters become tighter; and the length of ten characters decreases.

div.p: After the last column of the sutra text follows an additional column 111, with a Dhāraṇī spell. Its characters, too, are larger than the sutra characters, measuring 2.5 to 3.0 cm in height and 2.4 to 3.4 cm in width.

row.cell: 83 until col. 44; 88 from col. 45 to col. 105; increasing after col. 106; 120 in col. 119.
from 20.2 cm to 15.0 cm (in the last column, lower third)
ca. 0–1.0 cm, tighter in the last 5 columns

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: A zigzag-shaped fissure runs through the entire wall from floor to ceiling. It begins at the floor of the cave, at a distance of 60 cm from the rear wall, and rises vertically, but at a slight angle, till it is 75 cm from the rear wall and 120 cm above the floor. Cement has been applied to fill this part of the fissure. It then continues as a horizontal slit to the right, for some 130 cm, finally swinging upwards till it buries itself in the ceiling. This last leg of the fissure, which is 12 cm wide and also filled with cement, leaves to its right a trapezoidal piece of wall at the entrance that measures 67 cm at its base and 40 cm at the top.

The entire part of the wall below and to the right of this fissure has shifted forward towards the entrance of the cave by a width of slightly more than one column of text. Due to the wall’s poor state of preservation, the shift in the text is not clearly visible near the entrance; but it can be seen readily enough in columns 32 to 87 in segments 5-B to 13-B. The lower portions of the columns within these segments have all moved slightly to the right. In the plates, we have marked the shifts with black arrows.

Within the trapezoidal area below the ceiling at the cave’s entrance is a natural oval-shaped hole, ca. 155–160 cm above the floor. A smaller, man-made hole 55–60 cm from the entrance, in segment 4-A, lies outside the field of script, just below the ceiling. A corresponding hole on wall f is 78–85 cm from the entrance.

figure.head: 73號窟平面圖。雷魯卡根據馬本漢和王平國田野繪圖製作
Ground plan of cave 73; drawing by Lukas Ledderose after field map by MB and MS

row.cell: [合部金光明經序品第一] (一 would have been smaller in size): no longer visible; reconstruction by analogy with column 106; the beginning of chapter 1 (Preface), coinciding with the beginning of scroll 1 of the Golden Light Sutra.
金光明經壽量品第二: the beginning of chapter 2 (Shouliang pin 壽量品), in scroll 1 of the Golden Light Sutra.
金光明經三身分别品第三: the beginning of chapter 3 (Sanshen fenbie pin 三身分别品), in scroll 1 of the Golden Light Sutra.
金光明經懺悔品第四: the beginning of chapter 4 (Chanhui pin 懺悔品), in scroll 1 of the Golden Light Sutra (in the Taishō , chapter 4 is the first chapter in scroll 2).
金光明經卷第一: the end of chapter 4 (Chanhui pin 懺悔品), coinciding with the end of scroll 1, of the Golden Light Sutra.
金光明經業障滅品第五 二 (二 smaller in size): the beginning of chapter 5 (Yezhangmie pin 業障滅品), coinciding with the beginning of scroll 2 of the Golden Light Sutra.
自作教他見作隨喜身三: the last characters on wall d, from chapter 5 (Yezhangmie pin 業障滅品) of the Golden Light Sutra.
佛頂尊勝陀羅[尼咒]: the Dhāraṇī spell, following the sutra text.

list.item: A polished area, 202 × 220 cm, begins 7–9 cm below the ceiling and almost at the corner with wall d; it ends 12–14 cm above the floor and extends all the way to the corner with wall f.
The field of script is a vertical rectangle, 190 × 137 cm; it begins 14–17 cm below the ceiling and 11–20 cm from wall d; it ends 15–18 cm above the floor and 64 cm from wall f.
Like the last five columns on the preceding wall d, the horizontal rows of the grid pattern are lost. Yet the columns and characters are not spaced as tightly here as at the end of wall d.
To the right of the field of script is the damaged colophon of 733 in two columns.

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: The surface is even; large areas are worn; only the upper part in the field of script is clearly legible. On the far left, near the top of the wall, outside of the field of script, are the marks of dripping water. Shallow graffiti are scratched all over the wall.

row.cell: thin vertical lines visible in the upper part
口四意三業行……: continuation of the sutra text from column 110 of wall d.
金光明經讚嘆品第六: the beginning of chapter 6 (Zantan pin 贊嘆品) in scroll 2 of the Golden Light Sutra (in the Taishō edition, this is chapter 7 in scroll 4).
……今汝二子銀相等是: the last engraved characters of chapter 6 (Zantan pin 贊嘆品).

div.head: Layout
div.p: Covered entirely with figures of the One Thousand Buddhas in relief.

note.p:
Peng provides measurements of the cave, and calculates its volume at 11.868 cubic meters. He identifies the text carved on wall d as the Golden Light Sutra. He lists “the donated sutra from the tripiṭaka
檀三藏經” as though it were another sutra. He also transcribes the kaiyuan era dates on wall d (727) and on wall e (733). He was given this information by Hu Wenhe.

bibl.note:
Howard, following , also interprets “the donated sutra from the tripiṭaka
檀三藏經” as a separate sutra title.

note.p: Hu Wenhe gives the same dimensions of the cave as published in , but does not include its volume. He identifies chapters 3, 4, and 5 of the Synoptic Golden Light Sutra ( Suvarṇa-prabhāsōttama-sūtra ; 合部金光明經) carved on wall d, but does not identify the text carved on wall e because of its damage. He counts the number of characters in several well-preserved columns of the text, and gives an introduction to the synoptic nature of the Golden Light Sutra. He also transcribes the colophon of 727 on the upper part of wall d, and explains that the word tan 檀 here refers to the Sanskrit dāna , meaning “donation.”
Deng Zhijin writes that chapter 5 of the Golden Light Sutra 金光明經 was carved on wall d, and chapter 6 on wall e. He also mentions the colophon dated 727 on wall d.
Hu Wenhe repeats the information in his earlier publication, .

The authors provide measurements for the cave and for each wall. They list the number of columns and calculate the number of characters, and they identify chapter 5 of the Golden Light Sutra 金光明經 on wall d, and chapter 6 of the same sutra on wall e. They also mention the colophon of 727 near the top of wall d.

bibl.note: Zeng Deren transcribes the colophon of 727 in its entirety.

note.p: The editors of the Chengdu Municipal Institute of Cultural Relics and Archaeology describe the architecture of the cave in detail and include measurements of all walls. They identify the specific columns devoted to each of the six chapters of the Synoptic Golden Light Sutra (合部金光明經) on walls d and e, and give references to the Taishō edition. They also transcribe the two colophons of 727 and 733.

Lee includes cave 73 in her cluster V, which corresponds to section E. She mentions the Golden Light Sutra and the colophon of the kaiyuan era. Following and, she misunderstands the characters which precede the kaiyuan date (檀三藏經) as the title of another sutra.

Cao and Yu provide measurements for the walls and their surface area; and, based on rubbings, they count the number of extant characters: wall d, 8,188; and wall e: 7,521. They mention the Dhāraṇī spell on wall d, and transcribe the horizontally engraved colophon of 727 about the sutra’s donation on the same wall. They identify the text on wall e, as the sixth chapter of the Golden Light Sutra, and mention the colophon of 733.

In their comprehensive study of the Grove, the authors repeat the information in . In addition, they provide detailed diagrams of walls d and e, as well as a full transcription of the engraved sutra text, which they analyze with their own detailed scheme. They point out the variant characters (yiti zi 异體字) as well as differences with the Taishō edition.

Hida provides a general description of the cave. She identifies the surviving parts of the first five chapters of the Synoptic Golden Light Sutra on wall d, and she also mentions the Dhāraṇī spell after the sutra text. She writes that the sutra text continues on wall e, and notes that, in comparison with the Taishō edition, the sequence of chapters 6 and 7 is reversed on this wall. Hida further gives a transcription of the horizontally engraved colophon of 727 on wall d.

Deng Xingliang gives measurements and a general description of the cave, as well as a description of the Thousand Buddha motif on wall f. He transcribes the colophon of 727. He records that wall d is engraved with the first scroll and part of the second scroll of the Golden Light Sutra, and he transcribes the beginning and the end of the engraved passage. He records the continuation of the second scroll on wall e, and claims that part of the third scroll was also carved, transcribing the beginning and the end of the engraved passage.
